title: BFT Quorum Clock Configuration and Payloads
description: Schemas for configuring the BFT clock and validating inbound time observations.

Config.description: Startup configuration for the BFT Clock node.

min_quorum.description: Minimum number of valid observations required to form a consensus. Must satisfy formal BFT bounds N >= 3F + 1.

max_cluster_skew.description: Maximum allowable spread (in seconds) between the highest and lowest timestamps in a trimmed cluster.

max_drift_step.description: Maximum seconds the clock will correct itself in a single sync event (Anti-shock clamp).

max_total_drift.description: Maximum absolute cumulative drift allowed from the baseline OS clock (Anti-creep ceiling).

sequence_ttl_seconds.description: Time to live for sequence replay windows before they are safely expired.

fail_closed.description: If true, the system will hard-abort when safety bounds like max_total_drift are breached.

TimeObservation.description: A signed time payload from a trusted authority.

authority_id.description: Unique identifier (e.g., hostname or UUID) of the time authority.

key_id.description: Identifier for the specific public key or HMAC secret used to sign this payload, enabling zero-downtime rotation.

timestamp.description: The current time asserted by the authority, represented in Unix epoch seconds.

sequence.description: Strictly monotonically increasing counter to prevent replay attacks.
